question: Will the weekly dollar amount of loans made by the Federal Reserve under its liquidity and credit facilities have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-04-13?
resolution_criteria:
Resolves to the value found at https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/WLCFLL once the data is published.

The exact value on 2025-04-13 is unknown, but the most recent known value (as of 2025-04-03) was 4089.000. (The latest value released in Assets: Liquidity and Credit Facilities: Loans: Wednesday Level from the release H.4.1 Factors Affecting Reserve Balances.)
background:
The notes from the release: For questions on the data, please contact the data source: https://www.federalreserve.gov/apps/ContactUs/feedback.aspx?refurl=/releases/h41/%
For questions on FRED functionality, please contact: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/contactus/.  The notes from the series: Loans is the sum of "Primary credit," "Secondary credit," "Seasonal credit," "Term Asset-Backed Securities Loan Facility," and "Other credit extensions..  Additional background of the series:  1. the units of the series: Millions of U.S. Dollars.  2. the seasonal adjustments of the series:  Not Seasonally Adjusted  3. the update frequency: Weekly, As of Wednesday
